title: St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church
tagline: St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Bursouma's Orthodox Syrian Church is a parish of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church in Attuva (between Pandalam and Mavelikara) in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India. It is under the Chengannur Orthodox Diocese. Mar Bursouma is its patron saint.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Edappon
topPlaces.description: Edappon is a small town situated in Nooranad panchayat in Alappuzha district of the state of Kerala in South India. It is on the bank of the Achankovil river.

topPlaces.name: Nooranad
topPlaces.description: Nooranad (also anglicized as Noornad or Nooranadu) is a developing town in Mavelikkara taluk of Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located at a distance of 8 Km south-west of Pandalam, in Kayamkulam route.

topPlaces.name: Venmony
topPlaces.description: Venmony is a village in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district of Central Travancore area in Kerala state, India. Venmoney is 13 km south of Thiruvalla, 8 km east of Mavelikkara and 13 km northwest of Pandalam.

topPlaces.name: Pandalam Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Pandalam Mahadeva Temple is situated in between Thottakkonam and Mulampuzha villages of Pandalam in Kerala, India, on the bank of Achankovil river. The main deity of the temple is Lord Shiva, who is considered to be in a pleasant mood with his consort Parvati on his lap.

topPlaces.name: Kulanada
topPlaces.description: Kulanada is a village in the district of Pathanamthitta bordering with Alappuzha district in Kerala state within India. Kulanada assumed its name from the combination of words "Kulam" and "Nada".

topPlaces.name: Pandalam Bridge
topPlaces.description: Pandalam Bridge, popularly known as Kurunthottayam Bridge, is a concrete bridge constructed at Pandalam junction in Kerala, India. Kerala Public Works Department commissioned the bridge and opened for public at December 2016.

topPlaces.name: Pandalam Suspension Bridge
topPlaces.description: Pandalam Suspension Bridge is a pedestrian suspension bridge which crosses the Achankovil River in the state of Kerala, India. It connects Ayyappa Temple with Kaippuzha Sreekrishna Temple.

topPlaces.name: Valiyakoikkal Temple
topPlaces.description: Valiakoikkal Temple is the family temple of the Pandalam Royal Family. The temple is located at Pandalam in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, in the peninsular India.

items.note: Edappon is a small town situated in Nooranad panchayat in Alappuzha district of the state of Kerala in South India. It is on the bank of the Achankovil river.

Nooranad (also anglicized as Noornad or Nooranadu) is a developing town in Mavelikkara taluk of Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located at a distance of 8 Km south-west of Pandalam, in Kayamkulam route.

Venmony is a village in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district of Central Travancore area in Kerala state, India. Venmoney is 13 km south of Thiruvalla, 8 km east of Mavelikkara and 13 km northwest of Pandalam.

Pandalam Mahadeva Temple is situated in between Thottakkonam and Mulampuzha villages of Pandalam in Kerala, India, on the bank of Achankovil river. The main deity of the temple is Lord Shiva, who is considered to be in a pleasant mood with his consort Parvati on his lap.
Kulanada is a village in the district of Pathanamthitta bordering with Alappuzha district in Kerala state within India. Kulanada assumed its name from the combination of words "Kulam" and "Nada".

Pandalam Bridge, popularly known as Kurunthottayam Bridge, is a concrete bridge constructed at Pandalam junction in Kerala, India. Kerala Public Works Department commissioned the bridge and opened for public at December 2016.
Pandalam Suspension Bridge is a pedestrian suspension bridge which crosses the Achankovil River in the state of Kerala, India. It connects Ayyappa Temple with Kaippuzha Sreekrishna Temple.
Valiakoikkal Temple is the family temple of the Pandalam Royal Family. The temple is located at Pandalam in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, in the peninsular India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church?
faq.a: Stays in St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Bursouma's Orthodox Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Edappon, Nooranad, Venmony, Pandalam Mahadeva Temple, Kulanada.
